entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop species targeted for population breeding techniques.

entities.name: DIRECT SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding approach involving selection among landraces to exploit variability.

entities.name: LANDRACES
entities.description: Local crop varieties used as a source of genetic variability for direct selection.

entities.name: PEDIGREE BREEDING
entities.description: A conventional breeding method used to develop superior genotypes.

entities.name: BACKCROSS BREEDING
entities.description: A conventional breeding method used to develop superior genotypes.

entities.name: POPULATION BREEDING
entities.description: Breeding methods that utilize quantitative genetic theory to maintain larger gene pools and increase recombination.

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE GENETIC THEORY
entities.description: The scientific theory that enabled the effective use of population breeding methods.

entities.name: GENE POOL
entities.description: The total genetic diversity within a population, often limited by conventional breeding methods.

entities.name: LINKAGE BLOCKS
entities.description: Segments of DNA that are inherited together, which tend to accumulate in conventional breeding.

entities.name: RECOMBINATION
entities.description: The process of genetic exchange, which is limited in conventional breeding but enhanced in population breeding.

entities.name: RECURRENT SELECTION
entities.description: A specific population breeding technique used to mobilize genetic variation and increase recombination.

entities.name: GENETIC VARIATION
entities.description: The diversity of genetic characteristics within a species that breeders exploit for improvement.
